steps.title: Recognize the same line
steps.content:
The second equation is $4$ times the first equation.
$$
4(3x+2y=11)
$$
$$
12x+8y=44
$$
So any point on one graph lies on both graphs.

steps.title: Let y equal r
steps.content:
Use $y=r$ in the first equation and solve for $x$.
$$
3x+2r=11
$$
$$
x=-\frac{2r}{3}+\frac{11}{3}
$$

steps.title: Form the ordered pair
steps.content: The point is $$ (-\frac{2r}{3}+\frac{11}{3},r) $$ The answer is B.
